summary.intent: Fix a browser tool crash caused by LLMs sending explicit null for optional parameters, then ensure agent-created browser sessions are visible in the UI's Settings > Browsers panel, and add a 'New Session' button to the UI.
summary.outcome: All three goals achieved: null-stripping fix landed, BrowserTool and RealBrowserService now share a single BrowserManager instance, and a 'New Session' button was added to page-browser.js. All 705 tests pass and changes were committed.

repo: serde's #[serde(default)] only handles missing keys, not explicit null values — LLMs (MiniMax in particular) send null for every optional parameter, requiring a null-strip pass before deserialization
BrowserTool and RealBrowserService previously each instantiated their own independent BrowserManager, so tool-created sessions were invisible to the UI API
The workspace has a clippy deny on .expect() in tests — use unwrap() or proper error handling instead
Live browser viewing via CDP screencast with multi-subscriber broadcast is already implemented; cookie export/import and profile persistence (~/.moltis/browser/profile/) are also already in place
Navigating with session_id: None in BrowserManager.pool.get_or_create() creates a new session — the backend already supported 'New Session' creation before the UI exposed it

code.finding: Added obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) null-stripping before deserialization in execute(), and refactored BrowserTool to hold Arc<OnceCell<...>> so the manager can be shared with RealBrowserService
RealBrowserService updated to accept a shared manager cell from BrowserTool instead of creating its own
BrowserTool now created early in server setup so its manager cell can be passed to RealBrowserService; tool registration later reuses the pre-created instance
Added test for null-stripping deserialization of BrowserRequest; fixed .expect() usage to satisfy workspace clippy deny
Added createNewSession() function (POSTs navigate with no session_id to /api/browser/action), 'New Session' button in header, and updated empty-state copy to mention the button

workflow: Biome is used for JS linting/formatting; no issues were raised by the null-stripping and UI changes
Tailwind CSS rebuild was checked but no new classes were needed — existing utility classes covered the new button
Clippy is run per-crate to isolate pre-existing errors (e.g. moltis-voice) from newly introduced ones
The worktree pattern (plan-browser-viewing-HOHno) is used for feature branches

friction: Initial browser tool call failed twice with 'invalid type: null, expected u64' before the root cause was diagnosed — the LLM was generating null for timeout_ms even though it's a u64
Clippy workspace deny on .expect() in tests required a fix pass after the main implementation was done
Pre-existing clippy errors in moltis-voice and a CUDA-related error created noise when running workspace-wide checks, requiring per-crate scoping to verify clean results
Shared manager wiring required reading server.rs in multiple passes to understand ownership and creation order
open_items: Per-agent browser profiles are not yet implemented — currently a single global profile (~/.moltis/browser/profile/) is shared across all sessions
No mutual exclusion when multiple parties (agent + UI user) control the same browser session simultaneously — currently both can send input without coordination
The broader profile-per-agent architecture was explicitly deferred in favor of shipping the immediate fixes
